stories.title: Andreeva Bounces Back, Destroys Ruzic 6-1, 6-0 at WTA Rome

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: Mirra Andreeva answered Madrid heartbreak with a masterclass. 6-1, 6-0 over Ruzic in Rome. 54 minutes. Tour-leading 13 clay wins in 2026. She is not done. #WTA #ItalianOpen #Andreeva #TennisTwitter

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Four days after losing the Madrid final, Andreeva dominated Ruzic 6-1, 6-0 at the Foro Italico. No. 7 in the world. 13 clay wins and counting. Roland Garros is 18 days away. #ItalianOpen #WTA #Andreeva #Roland Garros

stories.title: WTA Rome Day 3 — Sabalenka vs Krejcikova Headlines Top-Seed Blitz

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text: Today in Rome: No. 1 Sabalenka vs. Grand Slam champion Krejcikova. Gauff vs. Valentova. Defending champ Paolini opens vs. Jeanjean. Three top seeds on court at the Foro Italico. WTA Day 3 is appointment tennis. #ItalianOpen #WTA #TennisTwitter #Rome2026

stories.title: Djokovic Draws Qualifier Prizmic for Rome Return Opener

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: Djokovic faces qualifier Prizmic in Rome R2. Six-time champion vs. the 21-year-old who beat Fucsovics to earn a shot at the greatest. First clay match since shoulder injury in March. #Djokovic #ItalianOpen #ATPTour #TennisTwitter

stories.title: Rome's Last WTA 1000 — Who's in Form Heading Into Roland Garros?

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: Roland Garros main draw is 18 days away. Rome is the final WTA 1000 exam. Gauff defending. Sabalenka No. 1. Andreeva 13-clay wins. Swiatek chasing form. Rybakina eyeing the double. Who is actually ready? #WTA #RolandGarros #ItalianOpen #TennisTwitter

## Freshness Justification
- All 5 stories are anchored to May 7, 2026 content: Andreeva result (confirmed today), WTA Day 3 matches (today's action), ATP Day 2 schedule (today), Djokovic/Sinner R2 preview (upcoming tomorrow), RG women's form guide (11 days to RG qualifying). No recycled stories from prior days.
## HIGH Priority Claims to Verify Before Dashboard
- Andreeva 6-1, 6-0 score (HIGH — confirmed by 2 independent sources)
- Sabalenka's Rome title record (MEDIUM — use as "eyeing a first Rome title" not "never won in X tries")
- Prizmic beat Fucsovics (MEDIUM — verify score if possible)
